newQuiz.stem: The factory used to dump waste into the river. ___, strict laws now force it to clean the water before releasing it, so the fish have slowly come back.
newQuiz.explanation: 前句是過去的污染問題,後句是現在有嚴法、魚回來的『好轉』結果。前後並非單純因果或對比，而是『所幸情況改善』，故 Fortunately(幸好)最貼合整段由壞轉好的語氣。
newQuiz.trap: ⚠ As a result 看似因果，但『過去污染』不是『現在嚴法』的原因；In the past 與句中 now 時間矛盾；On the other hand 需要兩個對等觀點對比，此處是『改善』而非平行對立，要讀完整段語氣再選。

newQuiz.stem: Neither the manager nor the workers ___ willing to accept the new schedule, which led to a long argument during the meeting.
newQuiz.explanation: Neither A nor B 的動詞採『就近原則』，動詞與最靠近的主詞 the workers(複數)一致，故用 were；後句 led(過去式)也定調整句為過去時態。
newQuiz.trap: ⚠ Neither...nor... 動詞看『最近』的主詞 workers(複數)→ were，不是看 manager；又後句 led 為過去式，故排除現在式 is/has been，須同時判斷『就近一致』與『時態呼應』。

newQuiz.stem: The new museum is well worth ___; it displays many rare paintings that you can hardly see anywhere else.
newQuiz.explanation: be worth + V-ing 為固定句型，表『值得做某事』，故用動名詞 visiting。
newQuiz.trap: ⚠ worth 後接動名詞(V-ing)，不接不定詞 to visit，這是與直覺相反的固定用法；被動的 visited 也不合，因主動表示『值得去參觀』。

newQuiz.stem: I called you several times last night, but you didn't answer. What ___ at that time?
newQuiz.explanation: 前句以 last night、at that time 指過去某『特定時點正在進行』的動作，須用過去進行式 were you doing。
newQuiz.trap: ⚠ at that time(在那個時間點)強調『當下正在進行』，用過去進行式；單純過去式 did you do 問的是『做了什麼事(完成)』，語意較弱，且與『正在做什麼以致沒接電話』的情境不符。

newQuiz.stem: Mike is much more careful than his brother. ___ of the two boys often double-checks his homework before handing it in.
newQuiz.explanation: 前句先提 Mike，後提 his brother；『細心、會反覆檢查』指的是先提到的 Mike，故用 the former(前者)，且 former/latter 須加定冠詞 the。
newQuiz.trap: ⚠ the former=前者(Mike)、the latter=後者(brother)；句意指『細心檢查』者是 Mike(先出現)→ the former，別搞反順序；又 former/latter 當代名詞須加 the，裸寫 Former/Latter 不合語法。

newQuiz.stem: She speaks English so fluently ___ many people think she grew up in America.
newQuiz.explanation: so + adv. + that... 為『如此…以致於…』句型，故空格填 that 引導結果子句。
newQuiz.trap: ⚠ so...that... 表『如此…以致於』，固定搭配 that；別看到比較語氣就填 than(無比較對象)，as 用於 as...as 同級比較，which 引導關係子句也不合此處邏輯。

newQuiz.stem: If I ___ about the traffic jam earlier, I would have taken a different route to the airport.
newQuiz.explanation: 後句 would have taken 是『與過去事實相反』的假設語氣，if 子句須用過去完成式 had known。
newQuiz.trap: ⚠ 主句 would have taken 透露這是『與過去相反』的第三條件句，if 子句必須用 had + p.p.(had known)；單純過去式 knew 屬於『與現在相反』，時態層級不對。

newQuiz.stem: The book gives some useful tips on ___ a job interview, such as how to dress and what questions to expect.
newQuiz.explanation: 介系詞 on 後須接動名詞，且 prepare 與 interview 之間需介系詞 for，故為 preparing for。
newQuiz.trap: ⚠ 介系詞 on 後接動名詞 preparing，不可接原形 prepare；prepare for 的 for 不能省(prepare for an interview)；how prepare 缺 to 不成句，to preparing 則 to 後不接 V-ing。

newQuiz.stem: By the time the firefighters arrived, the small kitchen fire ___ into a huge blaze that destroyed half the house.
newQuiz.explanation: By the time + 過去式(arrived)指出一個過去的時間點，火勢在『那之前已』蔓延成大火，須用過去完成式 had spread 表更早發生。
newQuiz.trap: ⚠ By the time 子句用過去式 arrived，主句的動作『更早完成』要用過去完成式 had spread;若用單純過去式 spread，無法表現『消防員到達前火已擴大』的時間先後。

newQuiz.stem: The plan sounds great in theory, but it is too expensive to ___ in a small town with so few people.
newQuiz.explanation: carry out=執行、實施(計畫)。前句說計畫理論上很好但太貴，後接『難以實施』最合理，故選 carry out。
newQuiz.trap: ⚠ carry out(執行計畫)≠ carry on(繼續進行已開始的事)；此處計畫尚未開始談『太貴難以實施』，用 carry out；put up with(忍受)、give up(放棄)語意皆不符。

newQuiz.stem: It was not the price ___ the poor service that made the customers complain about the restaurant online.
newQuiz.explanation: not A but B 句型表『不是 A 而是 B』；客人抱怨的不是價格而是服務差，故空格填 but。
newQuiz.trap: ⚠ not...but... 是固定相關連接詞，意為『不是…而是…』；別填 and(會變成兩者皆是)或 or，要從『made customers complain』看出真正的原因是 poor service。

newQuiz.stem: Tom was the only one of the students who ___ able to solve the difficult math problem without any help.
newQuiz.explanation: the only one of + 複數 + who... 結構中，關係代名詞指的是 the only one(單數)，故用單數動詞 was；又整句為過去語境。
newQuiz.trap: ⚠ 因為有 the only one，who 指『唯一那一個(單數)』，動詞用 was，而非與 students 一致用 were;這是『the only one of...』與『one of the...』判別的關鍵陷阱。

newQuiz.stem: Hardly ___ the bus stop when the bus pulled away right in front of us.
newQuiz.explanation: Hardly...when... 表『一…就…』；否定副詞 Hardly 置句首須倒裝，且主句用過去完成式，故為 had we reached。
newQuiz.trap: ⚠ 否定副詞 Hardly 開頭要『倒裝』(助動詞提前)→ had we reached，不可用正常語序 we had reached;又 Hardly...when 句型主句用過去完成式 had+p.p.，故排除 did we reach。

newQuiz.stem: The teacher asked the noisy students to lower their voices, ___ disturbed the class next door.
newQuiz.explanation: 此處需『非限定關係子句』補充說明前面整件事(學生吵鬧)，並作後句主詞，須用關係代名詞 which。
newQuiz.trap: ⚠ 逗號後補述『前面整件事』要用 which(非限定用法)，that 不能用於逗號後的非限定子句;what 不接先行詞、it 會造成兩個獨立主句而缺連接詞，皆不合。

newQuiz.stem: Online shopping has become very popular. ___, it has caused many small local stores to close down for good.
newQuiz.explanation: 前句講網購『流行(正面)』，後句講造成小店倒閉『負面影響』，前後語意對立，須用轉折詞 However。
newQuiz.trap: ⚠ 前『流行』後『害店倒閉』是正反對比，用 However;In addition/For instance 是補充或舉例(語意同向)，Meanwhile 偏『同時』時間並列，皆無法表現此處的正反轉折。

newQuiz.stem: Our team finally won the championship. The success was the result of ___ everyone had worked together for the whole season.
newQuiz.explanation: the result of + 名詞子句；表『大家合作的方式』可用 the way (that/in which) + 子句，the way that 為正確且完整的選項。
newQuiz.trap: ⚠ of 後需名詞性結構，the way that everyone worked together(大家合作的方式)合理;what 後接子句但 of what... 此處語意應為『方式』而非『的東西』；how that 連用不合語法，the way 後直接接子句雖口語可，但本題 the way that 最為標準完整。

newQuiz.stem: ___ being tired after a long day, the nurse stayed at the hospital to take care of the emergency patients.
newQuiz.explanation: 前『又累』後『仍留下照顧病人』語意讓步相反，須用 In spite of(儘管)+ 名詞/動名詞。
newQuiz.trap: ⚠ 前後是『雖然累卻仍留下』的讓步轉折，用 In spite of;Because of(因為)會變成『因為累所以留下』語意矛盾，Thanks to(多虧)語感正面亦不合，Instead of(而非)語意更不對。

newQuiz.stem: The harder the farmers work during the season, ___ they will earn when the crops are sold.
newQuiz.explanation: The + 比較級..., the + 比較級... 表『越…就越…』；前半 The harder 已定句型，後半須對應 the more。
newQuiz.trap: ⚠ 『The 比較級..., the 比較級...』是固定對稱句型，前有 The harder，後必為 the more,不可用最高級 the most 或裸寫 more/much more，要看見前半的 The harder 就鎖定句型。

newQuiz.stem: The new smartphone is reported ___ thousands of cameras and sensors during its three-year development before its release last week.
newQuiz.explanation: 手機是『被測試』(被動)，且測試發生在『上週發表之前』(更早完成)，故用完成式被動不定詞 to have been tested。
newQuiz.trap: ⚠ 主詞 smartphone 是被『測試』→ 被動;又測試在發表(過去)之前完成→ 完成式;兩者合一須用 to have been tested;to be tested 缺『更早完成』語意，to have tested 是主動完成式皆不合。

newQuiz.stem: Such ___ that the audience kept laughing from the beginning to the very end of the show.
newQuiz.explanation: Such + (a/an) + adj. + n. + that... 句型表『如此…以致於』；單數可數名詞 play 須用 Such a funny play it was that...。
newQuiz.trap: ⚠ Such 後接『a/an + 形容詞 + 名詞』(such a funny play)，再接 that 結果子句;若是 So 才接 So funny the play was。考點是 Such 與 So 後的語序差異，可數單數名詞前的 a 不可漏。

newQuiz.stem: We had expected a big crowd at the concert, but ___ a few people showed up because of the heavy rain that evening.
newQuiz.explanation: 前句『原本預期人很多』，後句因大雨『只來了幾個人』，前後對比落差須用 only(僅僅)強調人數出乎意料地少。
newQuiz.trap: ⚠ but 後與『預期人多』形成落差，須用 only(只)強調少;quite a few 反而是『相當多』，almost/nearly a few 不合英文慣用，要由 but 的轉折判斷此處應為『人少』。
